pattern: Every reachable lighting pattern repeats with a short period, so only the first few bulbs and a press count capped at three actually matter, collapsing a huge search into a tiny enumeration.
hints: Two presses of the same button cancel out, so only the parity of how many times each button is used affects the result.
The four toggle patterns repeat every six bulbs, and in fact the status of bulbs beyond the third is forced by the first three.

Enumerate the sixteen parity combinations of the four buttons, keep only those whose total press count has the same parity as presses and does not exceed it, simulate them on min(n, 3) bulbs, and count distinct outcomes.

edge_inputs.description: zero presses leaves the single all-on state
more presses than buttons matter, parity saturates

stress.description: random small-to-large n and presses to exercise the parity collapse
